content:
## Page Section / Title
1.1 Meerkat

## All Verbatim Text
- "Brand Identity Guidelines | by Sightbox"
- "1.1 Meerkat"
- "CUT OFF"
- "We've established some cutting parameters and ideas for integrating the Meerkat with the different and variable back shapes. The Meerkat's hands should always remain outward to suggest a more natural and integrated appearance."

## Visual Elements
- **Logos**: Three stylized meerkat illustrations with geometric, faceted designs using vibrant, multicolored polygonal shapes (e.g., oranges, blues, purples, yellows). Each meerkat is shown in a different pose (side profile, front-facing with hands clasped, front-facing with hands at sides), with dotted lines indicating "cut off" boundaries for integration with variable back shapes. No explicit clear space or minimum size rules are visible, but the focus is on the meerkat's form and pose.
- **Color Palette**: No named color swatches or HEX/RGB values are shown; the palette consists of vibrant, varied geometric colors (e.g., warm oranges, cool blues, purples, yellows) used in the meerkat illustrations.
- **Typography**: 
  - Heading: "Meerkat" (bold, sans-serif, large size, likely a modern typeface like Helvetica or similar).
  - Section number: "1.1" (smaller, sans-serif, lighter weight).
  - Subheading: "CUT OFF" (uppercase, sans-serif, medium weight).
  - Body copy: Standard sans-serif (likely the same as headings, smaller size, regular weight).
- **Imagery / photography**: Low-poly, geometric meerkat illustrations with a modern, digital art style. The mood is playful and vibrant, emphasizing clean lines and bold color contrasts.
- **Iconography**: No distinct icon set is shown; the meerkats serve as the primary visual element.
- **Layout grid**: The page uses a left-aligned text column (approx. 1/3 of the page width) and a right-aligned image column (approx. 2/3 of the page width), with ample white space around elements. Margins are generous, and spacing between text and images is balanced.
- **Geometric / decorative elements**: The meerkats are constructed from triangular and polygonal shapes, creating a faceted, 3D effect. Dotted lines (likely guidelines) are used to indicate "cut off" boundaries for integration.

## Page Layout Summary
The page is divided into two main sections: a left-aligned text block (containing the section title, subheading, and body copy) and a right-aligned image block (containing three meerkat illustrations). The text is positioned at the bottom left, while the images occupy the upper right two-thirds of the page. White space is used extensively to separate elements, creating a clean, uncluttered layout. The meerkats are arranged horizontally, with dotted lines connecting them to the text to emphasize the "cut off" integration concept.

## Design Rules / Guidance Visible on Page
- The meerkat's hands should always remain outward to suggest a more natural and integrated appearance when integrating with different and variable back shapes.
- Cutting parameters and ideas have been established for integrating the meerkat with variable back shapes (implied by the dotted lines and "CUT OFF" subheading).
